title: Empathy & Social Awareness
objective: Students will explore empathy & social awareness concepts including Vocabulary: understanding others, Other People's Feelings and Thoughts, Everyday Kindness and Care and more.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Vocabulary: understanding others!

Know and use the key vocabulary for understanding others — empathy, perspective, kind, fair, community, similar, different, and care — and understand that these words describe real habits of thinking and feeling
Let me show you something cool. Use the word 'empathy' correctly and distinguish it from 'sympathy' — e.g. 'Empathy means I try to feel what they feel'

When you sees someone who seems sad or left out, can they describe what that person might be feeling using words like 'empathy' or 'perspective' — showing they're thinking about the other person, not just themselves?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Vocabulary: understanding others: Use the word 'empathy' correctly and distinguish it from 'sympathy' — e.g. 'Empathy means I try to feel what they feel' Explain what 'perspective' and 'point of view' mean and demonstrate by describing how two people might see the same event differently
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Other People's Feelings and Thoughts!
Understand that other people have their own feelings and thoughts, and that these might be different from your own — a foundational awareness that not everyone sees or feels things the same way
Let's practice together! State that other people can feel differently about the same thing
If you loves dogs but their friend is scared of dogs, can you understand that their friend's fear is real even though they don't feel it themselves?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Other People's Feelings and Thoughts: State that other people can feel differently about the same thing Give an example of a time someone felt differently from them about the same situation
Today we're going to learn about Everyday Kindness and Care. Get ready for an adventure!
Show kindness and care towards others in simple everyday ways — comforting a friend who is upset, helping someone who has dropped their things, sharing without being asked, and saying kind words
Let me show you something cool. Spontaneously offer comfort to someone who is visibly upset
If a younger child at school falls over in the playground, does you go over to help or comfort them without being told to by an adult?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Everyday Kindness and Care: Spontaneously offer comfort to someone who is visibly upset Help another person with a practical task without being asked
Today we're going to learn about Similarities & Differences. Get ready for an adventure!

Notice and appreciate ways that people are similar to and different from each other — including appearance, family structures, languages spoken, foods eaten, and celebrations observed — and understand that differences make communities interesting
Let me show you something cool. Name several ways people in their class or community are different from each other

Can you talk positively about how families in their class are different — some have two mums, some speak another language at home, some celebrate different holidays — and see this as interesting rather than strange?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Similarities & Differences. Name several ways people in their class or community are different from each other Describe at least one thing they have learned from someone who is different from them
